message:
fix(tools): Anthropic API support, reservation conflict fixes, and mention parsing

The LLM module now detects Anthropic API endpoints and formats requests
using the native Messages API format (system as top-level field,
x-api-key header, anthropic-version header) instead of the OpenAI
chat-completions format. Response parsing also branches to extract
content[0].text for Anthropic responses. This enables direct use of
Claude models without a compatibility proxy.

The reservation index find_conflicts now correctly handles root-level
requests (no prefix) by scanning ALL prefix groups instead of only the
empty-prefix group. Previously, a root-exact reservation would miss
conflicts with entries in non-empty prefix groups, allowing overlapping
exclusive reservations.

The reservations module now rejects attempts to reserve the project root
directory itself (empty string after normalization) with a clear error
message, and changes self-conflict detection within a single request to
only deduplicate exact duplicates rather than running patterns_overlap,
which was overly aggressive and rejected legitimate non-conflicting
patterns in the same batch.

The macros contact_handshake now correctly identifies same-project
requests when to_project is explicitly set to the current project key,
not just when it is None. This allows the auto-accept fast path to work
in both cases.

The search mention parser now strips leading punctuation and quotes
before checking for the @ prefix, so mentions like ("@agent") or
'@agent' are correctly recognized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
